previous_statement: (GNS decomposition). For a state with active blocks I = {i : λ_i > 0}, the GNS representation is π_φ ≅ ⊕_{i ∈ I} id_{d_i}^{⊕r_i} where r_i = rank(ρ_i). The irreducible representations of A = ⊕ M_{d_i}(C) are exactly the identity representations id_{d_i} on each block M_{d_i}(C) (composed with projection onto the i-th summand). These are pairwise inequivalent for distinct i (they act on different-dimensional spaces, or if d_i = d_j they are inequivalent because they factor through different summands). The commutant is π_φ(A)' ≅ ⊕_{i ∈ I} M_{r_i}(C).
new_statement: (GNS decomposition for block-diagonal algebra). Let A = direct_sum_{i=1}^N M_{d_i}(C) with notation as in Node 1.7.1. For a state phi with active blocks I = {i : lambda_i > 0}, the GNS representation decomposes as pi_phi = direct_sum_{i in I} id_{d_i}^{+r_i} where r_i = rank(rho_i). Proof sketch: By the GNS construction applied block-by-block, the GNS of the restriction of phi to the i-th summand M_{d_i}(C) (with weight lambda_i > 0) yields id_{d_i}^{+r_i} on C^{d_i} tensor C^{r_i} (as in Node 1.6.3 applied to each block). The GNS of a direct sum state is the direct sum of the GNS representations of each active block. The irreducible representations of A = direct_sum M_{d_i}(C) are exactly pi_i := id_{d_i} composed with the canonical projection pr_i : A -> M_{d_i}(C). These are pairwise inequivalent for distinct i, as can be seen by considering the central projection e_i = (0,...,0,I_{d_i},0,...,0) in A: pi_i(e_i) = I_{d_i} (nonzero) while pi_j(e_i) = 0 for j \!= i, so ker(pi_i) \!= ker(pi_j), which precludes unitary equivalence. By Schur's lemma applied to the direct sum of pairwise inequivalent irreps with multiplicities r_i, the commutant is pi_phi(A)' = direct_sum_{i in I} M_{r_i}(C). [Inference: modus_ponens. Dependencies: 1.7.1, 1.6.3. External: Wedderburn-Artin theory, Schur's lemma, GNS construction.]
